titlestmt.titleproper: Preliminary Guide to the Leslie A. Falk Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Diane E. Kaplan

conventiondeclaration.abbr: finding aid created in accordance with manuscripts and archives' processing manual.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday July 11, 2019 at 09:01
Finding aid revision description not supplied.

did.abstract: The papers consist of correspondence, writings, and subject files which document Leslie A. Falk's career as area medical administrator for the United Mine Workers Health and Welfare Fund, teacher at the University of Pittsburgh Graduate School of Public Health and Meharry Medical College, and medical activist. The papers highlight Falk's interests in community health services, occupational health and safety, the delivery of medical care in other countries, and African-American medical history. These papers form part of the Contemporary Medical Care and Health Policy Collection.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Leslie A. Falk, 1993-1994,1996, and 2000. Gift of the Estate of Leslie A. Falk, 2005.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright has been transferred to Yale University for unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by the creator(s) of this collection. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Leslie A. Falk Papers (MS 1652). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The materials are arranged by accession.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers consist of correspondence, writings, and subject files which document Leslie A. Falk's career as area medical administrator for the United Mine Workers Health and Welfare Fund, teacher at the University of Pittsburgh Graduate School of Public Health and Meharry Medical College, and medical activist. The papers highlight Falk's interests in community health services, occupational health and safety, the delivery of medical care in other countries, and African-American medical history. These papers form part of the Contemporary Medical Care and Health Policy Collection.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

title.part: Contemporary Medical Care and Health Policy Collection

corpname.part: University of Pittsburgh. Graduate School of Public Health

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Received from: Leslie A. Falk, 1993 November 5

These papers consist almost entirely of subject files, including correspondence, writings, reports, articles, clippings, notes, and other material, which date from Leslie A. Falk's career as a medical administrator for the United Mine Workers Health and Welfare Fund, teacher at the University of Pittsburgh Graduate School of Public Health and Meharry Medical College, and medical activist. The papers highlight Falk's interests in community health services, occupational health and safety, the delivery of medical care in the United States and other countries, and African-American medical history. Further information on Falk's career can be found in the manuscript draft of an autobiography, which is housed in box 16.

did.unittitle: University of Pittsburgh. School of Public Health
Other countries: Britain; Eastern Europe; Central America; Kuwait
Black history and black medical history
Association of Teachers of Preventive Medicine
International Physicians for the Prevention of Nuclear War

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Received from: Leslie A. Falk, 1994 September 19

did.unittitle: International Physicians for the Prevention of Nuclear War
Blake Gould: Health Care for the Whole Person

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

did.unittitle: Early personal/professional files [includes "Health Care for All: A Life in Social Medicine"]
Professional files [includes reprints, correspondence, and subject files]
